In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. [[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - Kuroshio Town tourism information {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:黒潮町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - [[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - [[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|WD=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|WD=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|WD=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|WD=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|WD=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|WD=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|WD=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|WD=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - Kuroshio Town tourism information {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:黒潮町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|WD=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - [[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|qid=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|qid=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|qid=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|qid=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|qid=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|qid=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|qid=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|qid=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|qid=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|qid=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|qid=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - Kuroshio Town tourism information {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kuroshio)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:黒潮町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]

[[JA:加茂神社 (黒潮町)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153261}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|qid=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}Irino |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Irino no Matsubara, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} (national {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|qid=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}})}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ; also written 賀茂神社 / 加茂八幡宮 / 賀茂八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Irino, {{ill|lt=Kuroshio|Q1351626|qid=Q1351626|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=modern system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|qid=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|qid=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|qid=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|qid=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|qid=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|qid=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|qid=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|qid=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] - It is the general tutelary shrine of the Irino area, standing inside {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}}, a nationally designated {{ill|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|Q11414752|qid=Q11414752|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} * Co-enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-gū|1=Hachiman}} Alternative theories include: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} in ''Shingi Shiryo''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; unknown deity in ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}''{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; {{ill|lt=Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto|Q106412720|qid=Q106412720|1=Okamotsumi}} in ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}; and Ōkamozumi-no-Mikoto in ''Henkaimatsufu''{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === History === Foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is identified with the "Kamo Shrine" listed in {{ill|lt=Hata District|Q1132241|qid=Q1132241|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to tradition, Kamo Shrine originally stood at Kamo-yashiki in Irino Honmura, while Hachimangū stood at Hachiman-hara in Hayasaki; the two shrines later became one and moved into Irino Pine Grove{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Tosa no Kuni Shikisha-kō'' also says both shrines were originally in Honmura, though the date of unification is unknown{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. ''Nishiura Kaiken Nikki'' likewise says both were in Honmura{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. For the medieval period, tradition says Fujiwara Iemoto revived the shrine around the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|qid=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era ({{ill|lt=1467|Q6561|qid=Q6561|1=1467}}-{{ill|lt=1468|Q6564|qid=Q6564|1=1468}}){{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Also, the "Irino Ōgata no Gō Chikenchō" of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17 ({{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|qid=Q6759|1=1589}}) records shrine lands of 5 villages and 3 chō 6 tan in Irino Ōgata-gō{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Hōei earthquake|Q2001581|qid=Q2001581|lt=great Hōei earthquake|1=1707 Hōei earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|qid=Q6841|1=1707}}), the shrine buildings suffered tsunami damage{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. According to ''Nanroshi'', the shrine buildings floated in floodwater but returned to their original place when the tsunami receded{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A monument recording this tsunami damage remains in the precincts{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In earlier times, Rinzai temple Ōgata-zan Chōsen-ji served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}{{Sfn|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), honden.jpg|Honden File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sekihi.jpg|Ansei tsunami monument (Kuroshio town designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio), torii.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Kuroshio Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Kamo Hachimangū (architecture) - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47){{PDFlink|[http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/img/files/pv/sosiki/docs/sougousinkoukeikaku_honpen.pdf "First Kuroshio Town Comprehensive Promotion Plan"]}} Kuroshio Town, 2008, p.76 (link on Kuroshio Town website).. * Historic site ** Ansei tsunami monument - designated November 3, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/467}} ''Meiji Shrine Records, Prefectural Gōsha Vol.2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 467. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Kamo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kochi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Yatsunami Hiroshi|chapter=Kamo Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kamo Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Irino Pine Grove|Q131912175|qid=Q131912175|1=Irino Pine Grove}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kuroshio)}} * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/2981 Kamo Hachimangū] -
